column1: Groundhogs are a species of marmots
column2: which are rodents and closely related to squirrels. Groundhogs evolved four million years ago
column3: just when the first humans evolved. This was during the Ice Age
column4: which is why Groundhogs evolved such long hibernations.

column1: Groundhogs are famous in North America for Groundhog Day
column2: a holiday where the groundhog Punxsutawney Phil emerges from hibernation. If Punxsutawney Phil sees his shadow
column3: it's believed that winter will last another six weeks
column4: but if he doesn't then winter will end much earlier."

column18: furry animals with short legs and strong claws for digging. Groundhogs make homes under the ground in long tunnels
column19: and they like to eat plants and grasses. They can hibernate for as much as eight months
column20: far more than most animals. Groundhogs live across much of the eastern and central parts of North America
column21: as far north as Alaska and as far south as Alabama.
